Our growing portfolio brings together the capabilities of industry leaders including LetsGetChecked, Truepill, and Alto Pharmacy, to create a distinctive, unified force in healthcare. Together, we have the shared vision, advanced capabilities and talented teams to deliver next\-generation solutions that patients and healthcare partners need today and into the future. The Digital Experience, Brand \& Content Strategist is a senior, cross\-functional role responsible for leading the strategy, planning, and execution of Fuze Health's digital presence, specifically driving a major corporate website redesign initiative. This position bridges the gap between creative storytelling and technical execution to build a high\-performing, user\-centric digital experience. **Fixed Term 6 Month Contract** #### **Website Strategy \& Digital Experience Leadership** * Lead the strategy, planning, and cross\-functional execution of a major corporate website redesign initiative * Define website goals, audience journeys, information architecture, and content strategy aligned to business and brand objectives * Partner with UX, design, development, SEO, and marketing stakeholders to create an intuitive, high\-performing digital experience * Establish governance models, content standards, and scalable site management processes * Ensure digital experiences are optimized for usability, accessibility, conversion, engagement, and performance #### **Brand \& Content Strategy** * Develop and evolve digital brand storytelling frameworks across web and owned channels * Translate company positioning, product strategy, and campaign messaging into clear, compelling digital experiences * Create strategic content plans that support awareness, engagement, demand generation, customer education, and thought leadership * Define content hierarchy, messaging structure, and editorial direction across priority pages and audience segments * Ensure consistency in voice, messaging, and brand expression across digital touchpoints #### **Cross\-Functional Collaboration** * Partner closely with Brand, Product Marketing, Demand Generation, Creative, and Executive Leadership teams * Collaborate with subject matter experts and stakeholders to develop audience\-focused content and digital experiences * Manage agency and vendor relationships related to website strategy, UX, SEO, content, and development * Serve as a strategic advisor on digital experience best practices and emerging trends #### **Optimization \& Performance** * Monitor and analyze website performance, engagement metrics, SEO performance, and conversion trends * Identify opportunities to improve user experience, content effectiveness, and conversion pathways * Support testing and optimization initiatives across digital experiences * Develop reporting frameworks and actionable insights for leadership **Key Responsibilities** **Required Experience \& Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications: * 6\+ years of experience in digital strategy, content strategy, brand strategy, UX content, or digital marketing * Proven experience leading or managing large\-scale website redesign projects * Strong understanding of digital user experience principles, information architecture, SEO, and conversion optimization * Experience developing strategic messaging and content frameworks for B2B and/or complex healthcare organizations * Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills across creative, technical, and executive teams * Exceptional communication, writing, and presentation abilities * Experience working with CMS platforms, analytics tools, and digital marketing technologies * Healthcare, health tech, or B2B SaaS experience preferred **Salary Range** €65,680\.00 \- €82,100 At Fuze Health, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal\-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Our goal is to ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive. Fuze Corporate Inc. and its subsidiaries respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal information. Please read our Candidate Privacy Notice which explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect personal information about job applicants during the recruitment and hiring process.

title: Document Control Assistant
description: **About Ethos Quality Control \& Operations** The Ethos Quality Control and Operations Team plays a key role in supporting our engineering teams across all sectors. The team is responsible for maintaining high standards of quality, compliance, document management, and operational efficiency throughout the business. As Ethos continues to grow, we are looking for motivated individuals who are eager to learn and develop their careers within a professional engineering environment. This role offers the opportunity to gain experience across quality systems, document control, project administration, and business operations while working alongside experienced professionals. Our team values accuracy, collaboration, continuous improvement, and attention to detail. We provide training, mentoring, and career development opportunities to help individuals build long\-term careers within the business. **About the job:** As a Document Control Assistant, you will support the Quality Control Team with document management and administrative activities. Working alongside experienced team members, you will assist in maintaining internal records, preparing documentation, supporting quality processes, and ensuring information is organised and accessible. This is an excellent opportunity for an organised and detail\-oriented individual looking to develop a career in document control and quality administration. **About you:** * The candidate must be based in Ireland to be considered for this position. * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. * Excellent IT and computer literacy skills. * Good written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Positive attitude and willingness to learn. * Strong time management skills and ability to meet deadlines. * Motivated, adaptable, and reliable. * Fluency in English. **Role Requirements** * Assist with maintaining document registers and trackers. * Review documentation for formatting, consistency, completeness, and accuracy. * Support quality management and compliance activities. * Collate reports and supporting documentation. * Assist with general administrative tasks across the wider team. * Provide support to the Document Control team. **Key Knowledge** * Strong Microsoft Office skills including Excel, Word, Outlook, and SharePoint. * Excellent attention to detail and accuracy. * Experience with administrative tasks. * Experience gained through internships, work placements, or office administration roles would be beneficial but is not essential. **Minimum Qualifications** Leaving Certificate or equivalent. Recent graduates, interns, and candidates with administrative experience are encouraged to apply. **Essential Experience** No previous document control experience required. Full training will be provided. ****Why Join Us?**** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing** . **We offer:** * Flexible Fridays and a 9\-day fortnight working schedule — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A state\-of\-the\-art office in Charlemont, Dublin 2, designed to promote collaboration, innovation, and wellbeing. * A culture that values flexibility, learning, and teamwork, with clear progression pathways and support toward professional accreditation. * Opportunities to work alongside some of the most experienced engineers in the industry, within a forward\-thinking, technology\-driven consultancy. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times * 20 days holidays up to 25 days * Pension Scheme * Working travel out\-of\-pocket expenses * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training/mentoring / further college support * Support for Chartership * Subsidised Health Insurance Allowance * A Professional Subscription Fee paid by the company * Paid Statutory Leaves * Healthcare check\-ups and flu vaccinations * Gym / Fitness membership * Bonus referral scheme * Heavily subsidised social committee (5 a side, golf society, climbing club and regular social events) **At Ethos, we offer free Fridays every second Friday as a perk! Apply today and take the first step towards a healthier work\-life balance!** Here at Ethos we believe in equal opportunities and that each individual can bring something ‘WoW’ to our organisation. So if you don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, but think you have what it takes, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit. **At Ethos, our *vision* is to:** ***“Be the company people want to work with, and for”*** **Values** Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be **EPIC** \- for our people and our customers. Recruitment Agencies: Please do not reply to this job advert. Should Ethos require assistance with this posting you will be contacted by our Talent Acquisition Team.

title: Dublin Cabin Crew Opportunities (Dubai Based, Relocation Provided)
description: **Job Purpose** A personality that shines, the ability to adapt to any situation and make people feel at ease. These are a few of the qualities we’re looking for in our cabin crew. As the face of Emirates, you’ll be the person customers turn to for help and direction when they fly with us, so you need to be friendly, observant and able to provide the right support. Being a member of the cabin crew is so much more than a service role \- safety is our highest priority. You’ll need to lead confidently and take control when it comes to managing aircraft services, security, and safety procedures. This comes from the world\-class learning experience our crew receive at our state\-of\-the\-art facility in Dubai. **Qualifications \& Experience** **Here are some other things we look for in our cabin crew:** * You’ve had more than a year’s experience in hospitality/customer service * You have a positive attitude and the natural ability to provide excellent service in a team environment, dealing with people from many cultures * Your minimum qualification is high school graduate (Grade 12\) * You need to be fluent in written and spoken English * You’re at least 160cm tall and can reach 212 cm while standing on tiptoes, to enable you to reach emergency equipment on all aircraft types * No visible tattoos while you’re in Emirates cabin crew uniform (without covering them with bandages or cosmetics) * As Emirates cabin crew, you’ll be based in Dubai and will need to meet the UAE's employment visa requirements Aside from the requirements of the role, you should be determined to always perform to the highest standards, focus on being solution oriented, and be able to manage a demanding work schedule. You should have the ability to deliver an authentic experience to our customers. You’ll be culturally aware and reflect the Emirates personality \- professional, empathetic, progressive, visionary and cosmopolitan. **Salary \& Benefits** Your pay is made up of three components: 1. a fixed basic salary, 2. an hourly pay for operated flights and 3. an overseas meal allowance. Basic salary \= AED 4,980 / month, Flying Pay \= AED 69\.60 / hour based on avg. 80\-100 hours / month, Average Total Pay \= AED 11,244 / month (\~USD 3,100, EUR 2,600 or GBP 2,280\) \* These are approximate numbers for Grade II (Economy Class). Meal allowances for night stops are credited to the salary in arrears the following month. Hotel accommodation as well as transport to and from the airport is provided by the company. Join Emirates as cabin crew and enjoy a competitive, tax\-free salary, flying pay, profit share, and concessional travel. Benefit from comprehensive relocation support to help you settle in Dubai, along with free accommodation and transportation. You will also receive excellent medical, life, and dental insurance, and more. Additionally, your friends and family can enjoy discounted flights, creating lasting memories together.

title: Enablement Systems & Automation Specialist
description: LinkedIn is the world's largest professional network, built to create economic opportunity for every member of the global workforce. Our products help people make powerful connections, discover exciting opportunities, build necessary skills, and gain valuable insights every day. We're also committed to providing transformational opportunities for our own employees by investing in their growth. We aspire to create a culture that's built on trust, care, inclusion, and fun – where everyone can succeed. Join us to transform the way the world works. **Job Description** At LinkedIn, our approach to flexible work is centered on trust and optimized for culture, connection, clarity, and the evolving needs of our business. The work location of this role is hybrid, meaning it will be performed both from home and from a LinkedIn office on select days, as determined by the business needs of the team. The Go\-To\-Market Enablement (GTME) team empowers our customer\-facing teams through world\-class enablement experiences and infrastructure. This role sits within Enablement Tech Operations, a function of the GTME Centre of Excellence, and is critical to the operational delivery and performance insights of our global learning programs. **About the Role:** We are looking for an Enablement Systems \& Automation Specialist who thinks in systems, builds with intention, and can navigate the organisational complexity of a large enterprise to turn ideas into operational reality. This is a role with genuine scope and real visibility — one where you will have the opportunity to shape how enablement technology scales across a global GTM organisation. This is not a Learning Management System (LMS) administration role. It is a technical building role — one that treats enablement platforms as parts of a connected operating system and focuses on identifying manual, repeatable work and engineering solutions that eliminate it. The primary focus is automation and integration: from automating the learner experience through curriculum and event management workflows, to expanding proven frameworks and driving operational efficiency across the enablement ecosystem. Equally important is the ability to navigate enterprise complexity. Success in this role requires strong influencing skills to move initiatives through internal governance, access approvals, and policy holder requirements — often without direct authority over the teams involved. ETOps is a function that values craft, rigour, and momentum. If you are a learning and development technologist who wants to build things that matter and leave a real mark on how a world\-class enablement organisation operates, this is the role for you. **Responsibilities:** Automation \& Systems Building * Design, build, and maintain automations, integrations, and AI\-driven workflows that reduce manual effort and scale enablement operations * Automate the learner experience through curriculum and event management workflows, reducing manual effort and improving consistency across lines of business * Identify high\-impact opportunities for process elimination, not just process improvement * Evaluate and operationalise AI tools that improve how ETOps and the broader enablement function works Stakeholder \& Governance Navigation * Partner with Information Security, IT, engineering, and system owners to secure access, approvals, and technical support for initiatives * Navigate governance requirements and organisational processes to move complex, cross\-functional work forward * Influence stakeholders and maintain momentum in environments with competing priorities * Persist through ambiguity and structural complexity to deliver business outcomes Platform Operations \& Reporting * Support the configuration, governance, and integrity of learning technology and enablement tools across the stack * Contribute to reporting infrastructure and data flows that surface enablement performance insights for stakeholders and leadership * Build and maintain technical documentation, system governance standards, and knowledge resources * Scale and socialise best practices across multiple workstreams and global stakeholders **Qualifications** **Basic Qualifications** * 2\+ years of experience as a learning and development technologist, enablement technology specialist, or in a related learning operations or systems role * Demonstrated experience building automations or integrations using tools such as Power Automate, Zapier, APIs, or equivalent * Experience working with learning technology and enablement tools, including Learning Management Systems (LMS), and comparable enterprise platforms **Preferred Qualifications** * Strong documentation, analytical, and problem\-solving skills * Demonstrated experience implementing, developing, and scaling automations through learning technology and enablement platforms * Familiarity with AI tooling, prompt engineering, or agentic workflows * Demonstrated ability to influence cross\-functional stakeholders and deliver outcomes without direct authority * Experience navigating security, governance, and access approval processes in a large enterprise environment * Comfort operating in ambiguity and driving progress with limited oversight * Experience supporting large\-scale enablement or sales performance programs **Suggested Skills** * Workflow Automation * Systems Integration * Learning Management Systems (LMS) * AI Tool Adoption \& Operationalisation * Stakeholder Management \& Influence * Data Analysis \& Reporting **Additional Information** **Global Data Privacy Notice and Compliance Posters for Job Candidates** Please use this link to access documents that provide information about how LinkedIn handles the personal data of employees and job applicants, as well as the E\-Verify Participation Notice and the Department of Justice Immigrant and Employee Rights Section Right to Work posters: https://www.linkedin.com/legal/candidate\-portal.

title: Executive Assistant
description: Executive Assistant **Location:**Dublin, IE **Company:** Canada Life Group Services **Description:*** Full Time, Permanentposition * Hybrid role based in our City Centre offices **What we offer** We have embraced a hybrid working model for most of our positions, which means that you can enjoy a balanced approach of working from home for part of the week and working from the office for the remainder of the week. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive salaries and bonuses, robust Learning and Development support, excellent Defined Contribution pension and comprehensive Wellbeing initiatives and support to name but a few. Further details on our benefits package can be accessed here Benefits (life\-careers.com) **Role Overview** European Technology sits within Canada Life Group and encompasses the technology community across all European entities, including Irish Life Group, Canada Life UK, Canada Life Europe and Central Technology Services – Europe. European Technology supports the delivery of business strategy through strong collaboration, effective governance and modern, resilient technology platforms across Europe. European Technology is entering a new phase of transformation, with an expanded mandate covering technology strategy, transformation delivery, sourcing and partnerships, data and information governance, and enterprise\-wide change enablement. To support this agenda, we are recruiting an Executive Assistant to support the support to the CITO function. The primary focus of the Executive Assistant is to provide high\-quality administrative and organisational support to the CITO function. The role plays a key part in ensuring the smooth day\-to\-day running of executive schedules, meetings and operational activities, enabling leaders to manage their priorities effectively. Working as part of a wider EA network, this role supports coordination across teams and helps maintain efficient and well\-organised processes in a fast\-paced environment. **What you will help us to achieve** The successful candidate will: * Provide administrative support, including managing calendars, scheduling meetings and supporting effective time management. * Coordinate meetings, including room bookings, materials preparation, minute taking, and logistics to ensure smooth delivery. * Support the organisation of team events such as town halls, leadership meetings and offsites. * Assist with HR administration, including recording leave and maintaining absence records * Work with Executive Assistants across locations and time zones to support coordination of activities. * Coordinate travel arrangements, including flights, accommodation and transport, ensuring necessary documentation is prepared. * Provide day\-to\-day office and on\-site support where required (e.g. visitor coordination, meeting setup, catering). Process expenses, invoices and other routine administrative tasks in a timely manner * Act as a key point of contact for administrative queries, responding or directing as appropriate. * Log and track IT requests, following up to ensure timely resolution * Provide administrative support to team activities and initiatives as required. * Maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information with care and professionalism. **What you will need to be successful in the role** The ideal candidate will demonstrate: * Experience providing administrative or EA support in a busy or fast\-paced environment. * Strong organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritise effectively. * A professional approach and commitment to maintaining confidentiality. * Good attention to detail and a structured approach to work * Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools, including Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint. * Clear written and verbal communication skills. * A collaborative and approachable style, with the ability to work with a range of stakeholders. * Flexibility and reliability, with a willingness to adapt to changing priorities. * Sound judgement to manage tasks effectively and escalate issues where appropriate. **A****bout us** Canada Life Group is the top\-level European holding company for Great\-West Lifeco’s European insurance, reinsurance, and asset management companies, with business operations in the UK, Ireland, Germany, and the Isle of Man. European Technology sits within Canada Life Group and encompasses the technology community across all European entities \- Irish Life Group, Canada Life UK, Canada Life Europe, as well as Central Technology Services \- Europe. European Technology supports the business strategy and collaboration of technology across all European divisions. There are multiple IT units within European Technology, each with its own individual set of technology platforms. European Technology has evolved significantly and is now focused on bringing market\-leading speed, agility, cost efficiency, and risk mitigation to enable the delivery of strategies across the European businesses. To meet these challenges, a new strategy has been established, driving forward our commitment to innovation and excellence. The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. Where Agency assistance is required the Canada Life Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Canada Life by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role. Canada Life Group Services is proud to be an Equal Opportunities employer. We have created an environment of inclusion and growth to help people achieve their potential, to ensure they can bring their whole selves to work and to feel valued. We celebrate diversity of thought and perspective and want all of our employees to feel valued, respected and supported. We have built a culture that promotes equality and inclusion for all of our current and future employees and strive to ensure that our behaviours, processes and practices are aligned to this culture. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process please contact lifecareers@irishlife.ie and we will be delighted to ensure you are fully supported to be your best. CLGS supports Equal Opportunity and is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland.

title: Field Data Collection Specialist\Surveyor

We count features, take pictures, make videos, record speech, and scan areas for every detail you need to make more informed decisions. title: Financial Analyst

This role offers strong exposure across the business and would particularly suit someone with an interest in financial analysis, reporting, systems, and process improvement. **Key Responsibilities** * Drive and maintain monthly reporting and KPI analysis across departments and individual fee earners * Deliver detailed financial analysis to support strategic decision\-making * Collaborate with the Finance Management team on reporting and presentations * Liaise with Partners and internal stakeholders on finance\-related queries * Support and mentor trainees where required * Contribute to finance projects, process improvements, and system enhancements * Assist with ad hoc reporting and analysis * Present KPI performance insights at fee earner level * Act as a key liaison between Finance and IT regarding the practice management system * Support software implementations and continuous improvement initiatives **Candidate Profile** * ACA / ACCA qualified with a strong academic background * 1–2 years’ PQE preferred, though newly qualified candidates will also be considered * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities * Excellent attention to detail and reconciliation skills * Advanced IT and Excel skills * Experience with finance systems or implementations is advantageous * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Proactive, organised, and collaborative approach **The Opportunity** * Excellent salary and benefits package * Hybrid working model * Genuine opportunities for career progression and internal development * Exposure to a highly regarded international professional services environment To apply or discuss this opportunity confidentially, please contact Richard Minchin at Lex Consultancy.

title: Fluvial Geomorphologist - Hydromorphologist
description: **Overview** ------------ We are looking for a Hydromorphologist / Geomorphologist to join our team and work at the forefront of helping address the challenges of maintaining, creating or restoring water courses to/in good ecological status, ensuring that their form and function is incorporated into and/or protected from new infrastructure development, as well as restored and recovered from previous interventions that may have physically degraded them, e.g. channelisation, presence of weirs, culverts etc. We are undertaking an increasing number and range of important and exciting projects that involve assessing the current state of water courses and designing and developing methods and proposals for their restoration, maintenance or creation, dependent on the type of project. We are working for a range of clients such as water companies, community groups and other government and private sector entities and authorities. Building on this success, we are growing our team, and this growth could include you. You could help us to win and deliver work across our extensive client portfolio and enhance our reputation for excellence. As hydromorphologist, you will have experience of geomorphology or hydromorphology related project tasks in Ireland, the UK, Europe and/or internationally. You will work closely with our technical specialists who are based in our established teams across Ireland, both the Republic and in the North, and in the UK. We have a friendly and approachable team with a strong reputation for delivering excellence with multiple clients. **Your role** ------------- * With a degree in science, engineering or mathematics. * A post\-graduate qualification in hydromorphology or geomorphology, or a related subject, would be desirable. * You will have hands\-on, in\-the\-field, practical experience of hydromorphological assessment of river channels and water courses. * You will have experience as a hydromorphologist. * You will preferably understand fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology in relation to its role in determining Good Ecological Status/Potential under the Water Framework Directive. * Your experience will include geomorphology and hydromorphology, for example work on natural flood management, nature restoration, channel creation, maintenance and management. * You will have experience of delivering parts of, or whole, project tasks, such as data collection, data and information analysis, interpretation and drawing succinct conclusions and recommendations. * You will have good numerical, analytical, report\-writing and presentational skills and an eye for detail. * You will be highly motivated and proactive and enjoy solving challenging fluvial geomorphological problems within a high achieving team environment. * You will be able to communicate effectively with your colleagues to support collaborative working. * You will have valid full, clean, Irish driving license. **About you** ------------- * Demonstrable knowledge and practical use of one or more of the following would be an advantage: + River Hydromorphology Assessment Technique (RHAT) survey method for river water bodies. + The Lake Morphology and Modification Assessment System (Lake MImAS) for lakes. + Hydromorphological Quality Index (HQI) for transitional and coastal (TraC) waters, and/or Morphological Quality Index for Ireland (Quinlan and Mockler, 2020\). * Ability to survey, assess, appraise and report on the state of fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology in water courses and water bodies. * Knowledge and understanding of key fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology issues and their drivers, preferably in an Irish, UK or European context. * Experience of writing funding proposals, costing consultancy tenders and preparing technical and financial aspects of tenders is desirable. **Rewards \& benefits** ----------------------- Explore the rewards and benefits that help you thrive – at every stage of your life and your career. Enjoy competitive salaries and a brilliant range of health, wellbeing, financial and lifestyle benefits. Make the most of a myriad of opportunities for training and professional development to grow your skills and expertise. And combine our hybrid working culture, international remote working opportunities, and flexible holiday allowances to balance a great job and fulfilling personal life. Be rewarded. **About AtkinsRéalis** ---------------------- We're AtkinsRéalis, a world\-class engineering services and nuclear organization. We connect people, data and technology to transform the world's'infrastructure and energy systems. Together, with our industry partners and clients, and our global team of consultants, designers, engineers and project managers, we can change the world. We're committed to leading our clients across our various end markets to engineer a better future for our planet and its people. **Additional information** -------------------------- We are committed to creating a culture where everyone feels that they belong \- a place where we can all be ourselves, thrive and develop to be the best we can be. So, we offer a range of family friendly, inclusive employment policies, flexible working arrangements and employee resource groups to support all employees. As an Equal Opportunities Employer, we value applications from all backgrounds, cultures and ability. **\#LI\-UKI**

title: Forward Deployed Engineer
description: **At Poka, we’re transforming how frontline teams operate, learn, and solve problems.** **Our connected worker platform is purpose\-built for manufacturing, empowering frontline workers to share knowledge, collaborate in real\-time, and drive continuous improvement. Think of it as the missing digital link, bringing the human interface into the stack of the Smart Factory, generating the right data and insights that drive entirely new scales of process improvements.** **We’re proud to support global leaders like Nestlé, Bosch, Mars, and Danone in their digital transformation journeys and as part of the IFS family, our reach and impact continue to grow globally.** **You don’t just scope solutions \- you build what others can’t.** As a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) at Poka, you’ll work directly with leading manufacturers to identify, design, and deliver high\-impact AI and integration solutions inside real operations. This isn’t a traditional solutions or consulting role. You’ll own outcomes end\-to\-end. From discovery through to working software, helping customers move from idea to deployed solution in weeks. Working with Poka’s AI team, who have already designed and built agents, workflows, and prototypes that solve real problems using customer data, systems, and processes. Your role will be bringing AI to life in operational environments and driving acceleration with our global, enterprise customers. **What you’ll be doing** * Partner with customers to deeply understand their operations, systems, and priorities — and identify where AI and integrations can unlock value * Lead discovery sessions and technical discussions, shaping high\-impact use cases and defining realistic solution approaches * Design and build integrations between Poka and enterprise systems (ERP, MES, CMMS, etc.) using APIs and middleware tools * Develop AI\-powered workflows and agents (e.g., operator assistants, intelligent triage, document processing, anomaly detection) using modern LLM frameworks * Own delivery end\-to\-end — from scoping and architecture to implementation, testing, and handoff * Support strategic deals by contributing to pre\-sales conversations, workshops, and technical deep dives * Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and Services to refine solutions and translate field learnings into scalable capabilities * Contribute reusable integration templates and AI workflow patterns that can scale across customers * Travel to customer sites (:25–35%) for workshops, discovery, and key delivery moments **What you bring** * Experience in a customer\-facing technical role (e.g., Solutions Architect, Sales Engineer, Integration Consultant, or similar) * Strong hands\-on experience building AI agents, LLM\-powered workflows, or automation solutions — this is your core strength * Practical experience working with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic) and building real\-world use cases (not just experimentation) * Solid grounding in system integrations, APIs, and connecting enterprise platforms * Ability to quickly go from ambiguous problem → working prototype using tools like Python, Node.js, and modern AI stacks * Confidence bridging business and technical conversations, from frontline operations to IT stakeholders * Strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to adapt solutions to real\-world constraints * Comfort working in ambiguity and helping shape a new, evolving capability within Poka * Familiarity with manufacturing systems or industrial environments is a strong plus **Why Poka** * **Build, don’t just advise** – You own delivery and ship real solutions, not slide decks * **AI with real\-world impact** – Apply cutting\-edge AI in live manufacturing environments, not theoretical use cases * **Speed matters** – Your work goes from idea to deployed solution in weeks * **Shape what comes next** – The solutions you build directly influence Poka’s product roadmap and future AI capabilities * **High\-impact customers** – Work with global manufacturers solving complex, meaningful operational challenges If you’re someone who thrives on building, experimenting, and turning emerging AI capabilities into real\-world impact — this is where that work actually happens. **What we offer:** * Dynamic and collaborative work environment. * Generous vacation policy upon arrival. * Remote work policy. * A truly flexible schedule that allows you to adapt your work hours. * Benefits Plan offered from day one. * MacBook Pro and €500 allowance for your home office. * Generous referral bonus. * The tools, support, and opportunities you need to keep learning and progressing in your career. *Want to learn more about our culture? Click HERE.* **Please note:** An understanding of English is essential at Poka, as it is the language used during company\-wide meetings and many internal communications, ensuring effective collaboration and decision\-making. In addition, as Poka operates in an international environment, English is the primary language for communicating with our clients and partners, enabling us to better serve them and represent Poka in a professional manner.

title: French Billing Associate
description: **Before you apply to a job, select your language preference from the options available at the top right of this page.** Explore your next opportunity at a Fortune Global 500 organization. Envision innovative possibilities, experience our rewarding culture, and work with talented teams that help you become better every day. We know what it takes to lead UPS into tomorrow—people with a unique combination of skill \+ passion. If you have the qualities and drive to lead yourself or teams, there are roles ready to cultivate your skills and take you to the next level. **Job Description:** **FUTURE YOU supports customers and commercial integrity** Are you a natural when it comes to getting customers on your side? Do you also possess an interest in finance? If the answer to both these questions is yes, and you also have a talent for languages, you could be the candidate we’ve been searching for. We need you to secure the loyalty of our customers by showing them how swiftly we can resolve their billing issues. Every day, we manage the flow of goods, funds, and information in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. Come join us! **Employee Type:** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. **Employee Type:** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation.

title: French Payroll Specialist
description: **Description** Ryanair is currently recruiting for a **Payroll Specialist** to join Europe’s Largest Airline Group. title: Full Stack Engineer (.Net)
description: **Full Stack Engineer (.Net)** **6 Month Contract, Dublin, Hybrid** **About the Company** My client is a leading information and technology\-enabled health services business. They are dedicated to modernizing the health care system and improving the lives of people and communities. Serving virtually every dimension of the health system, they work with a diverse set of clients across 150 countries – from those who diagnose and treat patients to those who pay for care, deliver health services, and those who supply the cures. **About the Position** My client is looking to hire a versatile and results\-driven Full Stack Engineer with extensive experience in building and maintaining scalable, high\-performance web applications using .NET for backend services and React with TypeScript for dynamic, user\-friendly front\-end development. Demonstrates deep expertise in SQL Server database design, optimization, and integration, ensuring robust data management and retrieval across enterprise\-grade systems. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, develop, and maintain high\-performance web applications using **.NET** for backend services and **React with TypeScript** for dynamic front\-end development. * Develop and optimize **SQL Server** databases to ensure robust data management, retrieval, and integration across enterprise systems. * Architect and deploy secure, resilient, and cost\-effective solutions leveraging title: Full Transparency - Here's What Happens After You Apply
description: * **Full transparency: from our hiring process to our promotion structure** * **Full meritocracy: 100% merit\-based promotions \+ uncapped commission** * **We value your time: interview with us and receive feedback within 48 hours** **Access** is looking for driven individuals to join us as **Recruitment Consultants** \- no prior experience needed, full training provided \- as we push forward on our mission to become Ireland's \#1 healthcare recruitment agency. We're open to all backgrounds. It's 2026\. Candidates use AI to write CVs. Recruiters use AI to review them. Both ghost each other. We've all been there, and we're done with it. At Access, we do things differently. **Here's exactly what happens after you hit apply:** **The Process:** 1. **You Apply \-** Submit your CV. If there's a fit, you'll hear from me directly: no AI screening, no black hole. I'll give you a quick call to complement what's on your CV and get a feel for your story. If it's a match, your profile goes straight to our hiring managers. 2. **Interview Stage 1 \-** A conversation about your background, your understanding of the role, and your motivation. We're assessing relevant skills and how you think about recruitment. 3. **Interview Stage 2 \-** A short roleplay, live feedback, then another go. We're not grading perfection. We're grading coachability, and grit. 4. **Interview Stage 3 \-** A final chat with one of our directors. We're looking for alignment with Access's values: ownership, accountability, and ambition. 5. **After the Final Stage \-** We move fast. You'll have an answer within 24 hours. Accept the offer, sign the contract, and we get to work. ***Guaranteed: feedback within 48 hours at every interview stage.*** **Compensation:** * Competitive base salary \+ unlimited commission * **Year 1 average total earnings: €45K\+ \| Year 2 average total earnings: €75K\+** * Commission paid on every shift you book: no clawbacks, no limits * Top performers consistently earn well above these figures from day one **What We Look For** * 1\-3 years of proven success in any background (commercial, target\-driven, and fast\-paced environments are very beneficial) * Ambitious, coachable, accountable, and hungry to grow fast * EU citizenship or Stamp 4 visa Ready to start a process you can actually trust? **\+353 (1\) 572 1161 \| cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

title: Fund Accountant
description: **Job Description** A Fund Accountant will be required to perform the following tasks within the fund accounting or NAV department. They shall be executed within the guidelines and procedures provided by HedgeServ and under the supervision and guidance of a Fund Accounting Supervisor and Fund Accounting Manager. Reconciliation, trade capture, processing corporate actions and pricing are an integral part of the Fund Accountant’s role. They will be required to perform these core tasks accurately and in a timely and efficient manner. Every Fund Accountant will receive the requisite Induction Training in addition to both compulsory training (AML/KYC) and optional training. Additional, client\-specific training will be provided by the NAV Supervisor. **Key Accountabilities of the Role** The below list is not finite and may be added to. The combination of tasks required to be executed will vary depending on both client structure, client requirements and business needs. * Assist in the production of daily, weekly and monthly NAV, ensuring completion of specified tasks per NAV checklists * Perform cash, position and market value reconciliations between HedgeServ, brokers and clients * Identify and investigate break items on reconciliations in a timely manner * Escalate fund accounting issues and aged items to the Fund Accounting Supervisor * Ensure all positions are priced correctly * Verify corporate actions to ensure accurate processing * Prepare daily profit and loss reports and review for reasonableness * Calculate and process income and expense accruals including fee calculations and trading accruals * Comply with HedgeServ’s defined checklists, procedures and controls * Assist in the audit process if required * Attend and participate in team meetings * Collaborate and liaise with internal service departments to address miscellaneous fund issues. * Ensure pre agreed internal and external deadlines are met consistently * HedgeServ operates a client focused structure which provides a wide\-range of experience to all levels of employee, as such a Fund Accountant should work effectively within a team and adapt to changing requirements **Pre\-requisite knowledge, skills and experience** * 1\-3 years’ experience in a similar role * Accounting, Finance, Legal or Business Degree * Min. 2\.2 honors degree * Intention to actively pursue a professional accounting qualification (ACCA, CIMA, CPA, ACA) is not required but would be an advantage. * IT Literate * Strong verbal and written communication skills * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Strong attention to detail and well organized * Ability to work effectively in a time\-sensitive environment with defined deadlines * Ability to be flexible and work effectively both within a team structure and independently * Strong interpersonal skills * Demonstrates initiative while applying appropriate escalation processes * Demonstrates consistency in delivering assigned responsibilities * Take responsibility for assigned tasks * Demonstrate professional standards aligned with company values **We’re proud to offer a benefits package and workplace culture designed to support your health, career ambitions and life beyond work.** * 🏥 Full private medical insurance so that you can take care of you \& your family’s health \& wellbeing * 🛡️ Permanent health insurance/income protection as well as Life insurance, so that you can feel secure in any situation * Employee Assistance Programme available to you and your immediate family for support on a variety of matters * Defined contribution pension scheme with competitive employer contributions so you can look after your future * Competitive annual holiday entitlement so that you can slow down and take some time to rest * 🎓 Annual professional membership fee reimbursement, e.g. ACCA, CIMA * 🎁 Club/gym subsidy so that you can take care of your body * Discretionary annual bonus scheme * 💬 Referral Program – do you know someone who would like to join us? Referral bonuses are awaiting you! * 🏠 Remote/hybrid working opportunities, or from the office * 🧠 Tuition support for professional qualification exams that are relevant to your role including paid study and exam leave * 🎉 Sports and Social Club – many team outings, company parties, sports activites and charity projects * 💻 Support for workplace equipment and programs promoting mental health and wellbeing – because your comfort and health matter to us

title: GTM Data & Performance Analyst
description: **GTM Data \& Performance Analyst** Our client is looking for a commercially minded GTM Data \& Performance Analyst to help shape how their Go\-To\-Market teams make decisions, improve partner performance, and unlock growth opportunities. and enhance dashboards, reporting, and visualisations using Power BI * Use SQL to analyse large datasets and uncover commercial insights * Deliver partner performance analysis and GTM reporting * Identify growth opportunities, risks, and performance trends * Support projects across pricing, sales effectiveness, account management, and revenue optimisation * Measure the impact of AI, automation, and new technology initiatives * Partner with Sales, RevOps, BI, Data Science, Product, and Finance teams to drive smarter decisions **What We're Looking For** * 2–4\+ years' experience in recommendations * Experience within eCommerce, marketplaces, SaaS, consumer services, retail, travel, or hospitality * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills * Curious, proactive, **Package \& Benefits** 💰 €50,000–€60,000 base salary \+ bonus 🏡 Hybrid working – 3 remote days per week plus 20 days working abroad each year ✅ Health cover, pension, TaxSaver, and additional annual and Study Leave to support your development 🎉 "Unbox Your World" benefits including a YOLO day (take one day off at short notice each year) and a paid sabbatical after 5 years 🌍 Transparent salary bands and clear career progression pathways If you're passionate about data, enjoy influencing business decisions, and want to help drive the future of commercial performance and AI\-enabled growth, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Graduate Development Programme
description: Are you an aspiring engineer looking for a supportive environment to grow your career? As one of Ireland’s leading engineering consultancies, we invite you to apply for our Graduate Development Programme. As a fully Irish\-owned firm specializing in the planning, engineering design and delivery in the property, infrastructure, environmental energy sectors, with over 37 years’ experience in the industry, DBFL offers a unique opportunity to kickstart your career. By joining our team, you can become part of our legacy of excellence and innovation, working alongside some of the most skilled professionals in the industry. Our Graduate Development Programme is specifically designed to support your growth from graduate level to Chartership over a period of four years. From the moment you step through our doors, you will be immersed in a dynamic, supportive and collaborative environment that encourages you to learn and excel. You will receive hands\-on experience through a range of exciting projects and benefit from a structured training programme that includes mentorship, internal training sessions, educational site visits, external workshops, and self\-directed learning opportunities. This comprehensive approach ensures that you develop the skills and competencies necessary to thrive in as your engineering career develops. At DBFL, we understand that the journey to becoming a Chartered Engineer is a significant milestone in your professional development. As an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited organization, we are committed to providing you with the resources and support needed to achieve Chartership. Our programme aligns with the current requirements of Engineers Ireland, ensuring that all areas of competency and core skills are thoroughly covered. With our guidance and your dedication, you will be well on your way to obtaining this prestigious professional title within a minimum of four years. We believe that a successful career is built not only on technical expertise but also on a supportive and inclusive work environment. With a Silver Investors in Diversity accreditation from The Irish Centre for Diversity, DBFL is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, fostering a culture where every team member is valued and heard. Our modern, open\-plan offices in all our locations, promote collaboration and innovation, and our active social calendar ensures that you will have plenty of opportunities to build strong professional relationships and enjoy your time at DBFL. We strive to create a workplace where you can thrive both professionally and personally. To be considered for the programme you will need the following: * Bachelor’s or Masters’ Degree in Civil /Environmental Engineering * A good understanding of civil principles * Good written and oral communication skills * Excellent analytical, critical thinking and strategic skills * Good standards of accuracy and attention to detail * Good Computer skills * ‘A looking to learn’ attitude * Works well on own initiative and as part of a team Are you ready to take the first step towards an exciting and fulfilling career with DBFL? To be considered for our Graduate Development Programme, you should have a Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Civil, Structural or Transportation Engineering, strong analytical and critical thinking skills, and a keen eye for detail. We value good communication skills and a proactive, team\-oriented attitude. Join us at DBFL, where your engineering journey begins, and where you can become part of our mission, Engineering Sustainable Futures.

title: Graduate Mechanical Engineer
description: Graduate Mechanical Engineer, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now ***Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.*** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Co\-ordinating with site teams * Attend daily/ weekly meetings as required * Prepare reports/presentations as required * Assisting Intermediate and Senior Engineers * Ensure design complies with project specifications * Prepare technical submittals in accordance with project specifications and procurement schedule * Ensure materials / plant ordered are approved and in compliance with specification. * Work with BIM to develop construction plan and work packaging **Essential** **Selection Criteria / Person Specification:** * Ideally 2:1 Honours Degree / Expected 2:1 * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree * Good communication skills * Strong interpersonal and organisational skills * Adaptable and solutions driven * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Good organization \& co\-ordination skills * Flexible to travel * Enthusiastic and energetic **Desirable** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** Apply Now

title: Graduate Sales & Application Engineer
description: **Launch Your Engineering Career with a Global Industry Leader** Are you a recent engineering graduate looking to combine technical problem\-solving with a future career in commercial engineering and sales? SPX Cooling Technologies is offering an exciting opportunity for a Graduate Sales \& Applications Engineer to join our Cooling Towers \& Accessories team in Ireland. This newly created role is designed to develop the next generation of technical and commercial talent within our business. You will gain hands\-on experience working on real HVAC and process cooling projects, learning how to review technical specifications, support quotations, and help deliver engineered solutions for customers across multiple industries. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys engineering detail, wants exposure to commercial projects, and is eager to develop toward a future customer\-facing engineering or sales role. **Why Join SPX Cooling Technologies?** * Join a global leader in cooling tower and thermal management solutions * Structured graduate development and mentoring from experienced engineers * Gain exposure to HVAC, process cooling, and industrial applications * Learn both the technical and commercial sides of engineering projects * Clear long\-term progression into applications engineering, technical sales, and leadership roles * Supportive team environment with ongoing training and development **The Role** As a Graduate Sales \& Applications Engineer, you will support the technical and commercial teams by assisting with product selection, proposal preparation, and engineering analysis. You will work closely with experienced colleagues to learn SPX systems, engineering tools, and customer requirements while developing your technical confidence and commercial awareness. **Key Responsibilities** **Technical Support** * Assist with reviewing RFQs, specifications, and technical drawings * Learn to use SPX selection software (QTC) to configure suitable solutions * Support the preparation of technical proposals and submittal documents * Help ensure product selections meet customer and project requirements * Work with internal engineering and sales teams on project coordination **Commercial \& Sales Exposure** * Support quotation and proposal development * Attend customer meetings and presentations alongside senior team members * Develop understanding of pricing, project delivery, and market competitors * Assist with maintaining project pipeline and sales data * Build commercial awareness within a technical engineering environment **What We’re Looking For** * Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Energy Engineering, Building Services Engineering, or similar * Strong interest in HVAC, cooling systems, thermodynamics, or industrial engineering * Good analytical and problem\-solving skills * Ability to interpret technical information and engineering drawings * Strong communication skills and willingness to learn * Motivated individual looking to develop a long\-term engineering career **Development \& Career Progression** This role has been designed as a long\-term development opportunity. You will receive structured support and mentoring with a clear pathway into: * Applications Engineering * Technical Sales * Key Account Management * Commercial Leadership If you are looking for a graduate engineering role that offers both technical depth and future commercial progression, we would love to hear from you.

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Swiss Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and German * To have graduated from university in 2024, 2025, or 2026 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Ground Works  Technician
description: GeoBear is a global ground improvement contractor committed to the management of assets in the built environment, to enhancing people's quality of life and contributing to the decarbonisation of our planet. We provide non\-disruptive geotechnical ground improvement solutions and operate in the UK, Ireland, Finland, Sweden, Poland, the Baltic States, China, UAE, and the United States. **Immediate Opportunity for a Full\-Time (Ground Works) Technician in Leinster** (candidates to be based within a one hour commute from Leinster) As a GeoBear Technician, you will be a customer\-focused professional dedicated to ensuring the high\-quality execution of GeoBear solutions. Your role will involve meeting agreed specifications, deadlines, targets and consistently exceeding customer expectations. Professionalism and customer satisfaction with a total focus on health, safety, and environmental concerns are key priorities for this position. You will be assisting the site team in the delivery of specialist geotechnical procedures and following scopes of work defined by highly engineered design process. **This role involves physical labour, such as drilling, requires a HGV driving licence and can involve time working away from home as required (food and accommodation covered).** **Experience:** * Permanent full time position with abundant opportunities for career growth * Over 2 years of experience in construction, preferably in groundworks, geotechnical works, civil engineering or house building. Plumbing experience for drainage protection would also be beneficial. * Ability to operate drilling machinery and other tools as required. * Ability to follow written and verbal instructions from the on\-site management team. * Commitment to working safely in accordance with site health and safety procedures and policies. **Benefits:** * Competitive rates paid fortnightly * Additional travel and overnight allowances * Paid accommodation for overnight trips * Pension scheme * PPE and GeoBear uniform supplied * Training opportunities are provided as required as an essential part of employee development. * Opportunities for career progression and promotion To learn more about roles in the sustainable geotechnical asset management sector, visit our company website at www.geobear.com .

title: HR Administrator - Graduate Level

title: HR Business Partner (Band 6)
description: Vacancy Number: 28619 Category: Human Resources Job Type: Full\-time **Position description** The capability of our people is core to ESB’s success. HR works in partnership with the business to ensure we can attract, develop, motivate, and retain the talent required to deliver our Net Zero strategy. As a key member of the Generation Trading HR Team, the HR Business Partner will positively impact colleagues and leaders in the organisation whilst ensuring HR processes and programmes are implemented in a manner that adds value to the business. HR Business Partners advise, coach and support management teams on people\-related initiatives and matters. At ESB, you’ll join a purpose‑driven organisation committed to innovation, sustainability and continuous development. This role offers the chance to influence meaningful change and grow your HR career in a supportive, collaborative environment. **Key Responsibilities** * **Business Partnering \& Stakeholder Management:** Build trusted relationships across the business to understand business priorities, workforce needs and people related challenges. Work collaboratively with HR Centres of Excellence and People Operations to deliver people initiatives and support continuous improvement. * **People Strategy, Planning \& Delivery:** Develop HR plans, targets \& KPIs with business line managers. Use insights from the analysis of people related data and leading external practices to influence and inform business decisions. Take lead responsibility for key strategic HR areas on behalf of the HR Team for GT, e.g. succession. * **Talent \& Capability:** Identify the resourcing and capability needs of the business through succession and business planning processes. Work with Talent Acquisition (TA) to meet needs of the business. * **Employee Relations:** Coach people managers on Employee Relations issues in accordance with ESB Policies and Procedures. Ensure adherence to HR compliance and risk management frameworks * **Change \& Culture Enablement:** Support organisational change initiatives/projects and facilitate a high performing organisational culture through effective people management and development. Support the implementation of Group\-wide HR initiatives within GT. * As an experienced member of team, support knowledge\-sharing within the team. Contribute to a positive, supportive team environment, by living our ESB values \& behaviours. **Experience and Qualifications** **Essential** * 7 years’ relevant work experience. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to build and develop key relationships with managers and other stakeholders. * Ability to work cross functionally with colleagues on a variety of projects. * Proven analytical, judgement and decision\-making skills * Attention to detail, with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. **Desirable** * Background in HR and knowledge of HR Practice, Policies and Procedures * An understanding of relevant legislation and procedures * Membership of CIPD **Location** This position will be based in an ESB Head Office Dublin, however travel to locations such as Moneypoint and other Asset locations will be a regular feature of the role. Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration **at least two days per week**. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. **Reporting To** Senior HR Business Partner **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €71,976 \- €84,677 per annum **Closing Date** 25th June 2026 **Interview** Candidates who are shortlisted for interview will complete a Behavioural Interview based on each of the following Behaviours, which underpin how we work at ESB and are critical to delivering our strategy: * Accountable and Ethical * Collaborative * Growing and Adaptive * Proactive * Respectful and Supportive More information available on Our People Promise **Notes** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the Republic of Ireland prior to commencement of employment. While initial appointment will be to this position, rotation within Generation and Trading may be a feature of the role. **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. title: HSE Advisor
description: **Building a sustainable tomorrow** Working at BAM means delivering projects that make a difference. From the major infrastructure that keeps society moving, to the social housing, schools, and community spaces that shape daily life. We don’t just talk about social value. We embed it into every build. We are recruiting a **Project Health,Safety \& Environmental Advisor** to join our Carlow based project. The role of the Project HSE Advisor is to guide, support and advise Project Managers and other relevant personnel on the setting up, implementation and continuous maintenance of HSE systems and standards on site in accordance with BAM Safety and Environmental Management Systems. **Making Possible** * Promote safe working practices and ensure the implementation of company HSE policy and procedures on site. * Carry out regular site HSE inspections and report back findings to relevant personal. * Ensure that the project has been provided with the relevant HSE documentation. * Ensure that HSE project plans meet company standards. * Ensure that HSE issues are discussed at project review meetings. * Keep and maintain clear records to comply with company internal and external audit standards. * Deliver HSE training where appropriate and as agreed with the Project Manager. * Keep up\-to\-date on Health, Safety and Environmental legislation. * Communicate Demonstrate and Promote BAM HSE ethos, values and concepts. * Become a Role Model for Safe Behaviour. * Carry out other relevant duties as required from time to time. * Liaising with the relevant legal authorities (i.e. H.S.A, EPA etc.) * Knowledge of all legal requirements especially in regard to the PSCS role and duties. * Ensure all accidents and dangerous incidents are thoroughly investigated and reports provided to the HSE \& * Sustainability Manager detailing causes, conclusions and recommendations to avoid recurrence. * Provide the HSE Department with relevant HSE information on a quarterly basis. **What’s in it for you?** Opportunities! Opportunities to work with a great team on some of Irelands largest, dynamic and most exciting construction and civil engineering projects. Opportunity to develop your skills to the highest industry standards at one of the country's largest construction firms. We are committed to our employees and offer strong competitive salaries along with benefits such as: * 8% pension contribution. * Private Health Insurance. * Dental Plan. * 26 days annual leave, incremental with years service. * 2 Wellbeing days annually. * Paid maternity \& paternity leave. * Learning \& Development opportunities \- internal progression is encouraged and fostered. * Company vehicles (role dependent). * .....and many more great perks. **What do you bring to the role?** * Level 7 qualification in Health and Safety. * 3 years successful postgraduate experience in Project HSE Management. * Thorough knowledge of ISO: 45001 and ISO: 14001 standards. * Working towards graduate membership of IOSH. * Ability to deliver internal Health and Safety management courses. * Ability to advise on HSE issues on a day to day basis on site. * Ability to conduct standard accident/incident investigations. * Ability to write HSE site reports. * Qualification in Environmental Management is desirable. **About BAM** Building a sustainable tomorrow. That’s our mission and our promise at BAM. It’s how we engineer vital infrastructure and construct high\-quality buildings as one of the largest construction companies in Europe. We strive to create an environment where everybody feels welcome and valued. We’re on an exciting journey to employ the best talent to join us regardless of social background, race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability or other characteristics. **The application process** BAM is committed to ensuring a fully inclusive recruitment and onboarding process, so if at any time you feel we need to do something to make it more accessible to you, do not hesitate to let us know. Click the link to apply and Join us in making possible \#ie1 * Please note, CV's will not be accepted directly by email.

development** and career **advancement opportunities** .
