title: Host front desk
description: **About Queensway** Queensway Group is a family\-owned business founded by entrepreneurs and renowned for its warmth, sincerity, and quality of service. We invest in real estate which we develop into operating business in the hospitality sector, from coffee shops to hotels, residential property, and a member’s club. The brands we operate include KFC, Starbucks, Point A Hotels, Montagu Place, Sloane Place, and the Sloane Club. With our entrepreneurial approach and extensive network, we can respond to opportunities and drive growth in our businesses. At Queensway we never stand still, and our agility is key to our success. This, together with our experience in real estate development, means we can effectively transform assets which are then operated by skilled and dedicated teams across the UK, Europe, and East Africa. **About Point A** At Point A we are defined by our state of mind, not the state of the market. When we work together, we believe anything is possible. Our strength lies in our ability to think differently and use what we have innovatively. We want to defy the status quo and challenge the leaders in our sector with our brand, products, and service. In everything we do, we are thoughtful, humble, honest, and true to our personal values. We deliver heartfelt hospitality to all our guests, no matter their budget. Since 2017 this approach has been key to our becoming one of the most successful new hotel brands, but we need fine folk to help us continue the story and write more of the successful paragraphs. **The Role** We are currently seeking **Reception** team members to support our team and guests at Point **A (ADD LOCATION)** The role’s primary focus is on ensuring every single guest has an unforgettable experience at our hotel. **Key Requirements.** * Excellent customer service and problem\-solving skills. * Auditing and banking end of shift reconciliations. * Supporting other teams in the hotel and ensuring effective shift handovers. * Passion for service and delivering unforgettable experiences to our customers. * Positive attitude and drive. * Passion for developing yourself * Kindness, honesty and care for the surrounding community. * Good understanding of the industry and what it takes to be the best. * Imagination and ability to maximise business potential. Must have right to work in UK and be able to commute to property locations. **We will offer you:** * + Attractive salary, 40h a week contract. + Queensway Reward \& Recognition Scheme + Annual Awards Ceremony in London + Fun, friendly, and supportive work environment + Professional development and career progression + Career opportunities across the group including coffee houses and support office + Great team building initiatives and social events + Friends and Family Rates at all our properties + 30 days of annual leave **Salary: €14\.50 per hour**

title: IT & Digital Business Partner
description: The EMP IT \& Digital team is responsible for delivering the EMP IT \& Digital Strategy required by the business to support the strategic objectives of EMP and ESB, and to manage the implementation of strategy in a cost\-effective and value\-driven manner. The current EMP IT \& Digital landscape comprises a complex and wide variety of technical engineering and project management applications enabling EMP to meet highly complex engineering challenges and deliver ESB Networks and Generation Trading large scale capital projects. EMP recently went through reorganisation enabling us to be strategically placed to deliver on the significant increased volume and scale of capital projects. In line with this the EMP IT \& Digital team are similarly being restructured, with the opportunity now for an energetic and experienced person to fill our cross\-business partner role. **Key Responsibilities** Key responsibilities for this role include: * Work closely with BU Senior Management Teams to define and drive out the implementation of the Digital and IT strategies * Cross\-Directorate view (i.e. interacting with other Senior IT BPs and teams) * Consults with functional (CIO and IT Delivery) colleagues before making recommendations to BU colleagues * Define impact of emerging technology on the strategic direction of Major Projects and contribute to all areas of Engineering \& Major Projects * Responsible for the identification and submission of IT investments into the overall BU governance process and their subsequent delivery for EMP * Point of escalation for issues raised by the business or SMT members across EMP * Proactive monitoring of quality and delivery of IT services for EMP * As delegated by the Senior IT \& Digital business partner, responsible for the oversight of corporately driven IT initiatives for EMP * Support the wider IT \& Digital team in cross BU initiatives * Supporting EMP’s IT \& Digital Governance business partner with GDPR related activities across EMP, such as SAR’s (Subject Access requests), DPIA’s (Data Protection Impact Assessments) and ROP (Records of processing) Reviews **Knowledge, Skills And Experience** * Candidates must have 7 years relevant experience * Ability to analyse and explain IT matters in business terms * Must be an effective team player across all levels within the organisation * Excellent understanding of the ESB IT Delivery \& CIO organisations * Self\-motivator and problem solver, with a proven delivery track record * Excellent communications, facilitation \& interpersonal skills * Ability to influence others to move toward consensus * Ability to work in a dynamic and changing environment * Corporate\-wide view and understanding of ESB business units **Qualifications** **Essential** * A recognised third level qualification in an IT/Computer Science or Business discipline or equivalent work experience is essential. * In addition the role requires five to seven years’ experience in a IT role. **Desirable** * Knowledge and experience of enterprise wide technologies and infrastructure employed across ESB * Digital \& Cyber Security Technologies * Working knowledge of GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) **Location** This position will be based in ESB Engineering \& Major Projects, The Sidings Building Grand Canal Quay, Dublin 2\. However, from time to time overseas and in country travel may be required. **Reporting to** The successful candidate will report to the Engineering \& Major Projects Senior IT \& Digital Business Partner. **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €71,000 \- €84,000 Per annum **Closing Date** 26th June 2026 **Interview** Candidates who are shortlisted for interview will complete a Behavioural Interview based on each of the following Behaviours, which underpin how we work at ESB and are critical to delivering our strategy: * Accountable and Ethical * Collaborative * Growing and Adaptive * Proactive * Respectful and Supportive More information available on Our People Promise **Notes** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the Republic of Ireland / UK prior to commencement of employment.

title: IT Security Engineer
description: **About Us** GOAL has responded to the world’s major humanitarian crises since its founding in 1977, working with vulnerable communities in 14 countries across the globe with a dedicated team of personnel. While GOAL continues to be an agile first\-responder to humanitarian crises, it is also committed to working with vulnerable communities to help them survive crises and support them on the road to recovery. **Job Purpose** We are seeking an experienced IT Security Engineer to join our IT Services team, this role offers the opportunity to work across a broad range of security activities, from risk management and incident response to strengthening controls and supporting global IT teams. Reporting to the Global IT Services Manager, you will work closely with internal stakeholders to enhance security practices, respond to emerging threats, and ensure a consistent and robust approach to information security. This is an excellent opportunity for an IT professional with a proactive mindset and a strong willingness to learn and adapt to new challenges. **Information Security \& Risk Management** * Support the lifecycle management of information security policies and documentation, ensuring they remain accurate and up to date * Identify and implement improvements in information security risk management across GOAL’s systems * Review and recommend security controls, systems, and enhancements to ensure consistent protection across all IT environments * Investigate and resolve security exceptions, incidents, and alerts as required * Deliver and manage organisation\-wide information security awareness programmes * Assess the security of new IT products and services, including conducting security assessments * Provide security input and support to IT projects as required **Global IT Support** * Act as an escalation point for in country IT technicians via email and remote access * Collaborate with in\-country IT teams to resolve cybersecurity issues * Provide guidance and support to IT teams, SMTs, and users across global offices **Incident Management** * Log, track, and manage cybersecurity incidents through to resolution using the Service Desk system * Provide timely updates to users and maintain accurate ticket records * Escalate incidents to relevant internal teams or external partners, ensuring effective handover **Documentation \& Reporting** * Document processes and procedures clearly and consistently * Develop user and support documentation as needed * Maintain the knowledge base with solutions and updates * Keep accurate records of hardware and software assets * Provide regular updates to the IT Services Manager **Requirements:** * Some previous experience in a similar IT services/security role * Previous experience processing and prioritising IT services tickets * Ability to adapt to evolving IT environments and learn new technologies * Experience in managing technology adoption projects * Ability to write and maintain IT services catalogue documentations * Strong customer service experience and ability to deal with diverse users * Microsoft certification or extensive work experience in Microsoft products. **Desirable** : * ITIL certification * Experience in developing user training materials * Experience in developing power apps solutions * CompTIA Security\+ certification * CompTIA Network\+ certification ***Why Join GOAL?*** * Make a real impact by supporting critical humanitarian work. * Gain global exposure working with in\-country IT teams. * Play a key role in strengthening security across the organisation. * Grow your skills in a dynamic, purpose\-driven environment. *\*\*GOAL offers staff flexible working with our hybrid model, we require our HQ employees to* ***work 2 days*** *per week from our office in Dun Laoghaire, Dublin\*\** We strongly encourage you to apply even if you do not meet every single requirement listed. At GOAL we welcome people of all abilities and want to ensure that our hiring and interview process meets the needs of all applicants. If you require a reasonable accommodation to make your application or interview experience a great one, please just contact our Recruitment Team at **recruitment.hq@goal.ie**

title: IT Support & Implementation Engineer
description: A leading airport technology provider is seeking an IT Support and Implementation Engineer to join its growing team based at Dublin Airport, with flexible hybrid working and global travel opportunities. This role combines technical support with hands\-on system implementation, giving you the chance to directly impact airport operations worldwide. You’ll join a collaborative and inclusive environment offering strong career development, extensive training, flexible working, share options, and excellent benefits. IT Support and Implementation Engineer **Salary:** €40,000 \- €50,000 **Location:** Dublin Airport (Initially 4 days onsite with potential business travel) A leading airport technology provider is seeking an IT Support and Implementation Engineer to join its growing team based at Dublin Airport, with flexible remote working and global travel opportunities. This role combines technical support with hands\-on system implementation, giving you the chance to directly impact airport operations worldwide. You’ll join a collaborative and inclusive environment offering strong career development, extensive training, flexible working, share options, and excellent benefits. **What You'll Do** * Diagnose and resolve technical issues across hardware, networking, and SaaS products for airport and airline customers. * Work within a global follow\-the\-sun support model, collaborating with international teams. * Manage and prioritise incoming support requests across multiple customers and products. * Take ownership of issues through to resolution, maintaining clear communication with clients throughout. * Support network infrastructure including switches, VLANs, IP routing, firewalls, and end\-user equipment. * Assist with software deployments and onsite implementations at customer locations globally. * Deliver product training to airport IT teams covering operations, troubleshooting, and maintenance. * Provide regular project and deployment updates to internal teams and stakeholders. * Image and configure PCs, servers, and hardware while following change management procedures. * Create and maintain documentation, knowledge base articles, and operational runbooks. **What You Bring** * Experience in technical support or systems implementation within SaaS or technology\-led environments. * Strong customer service and communication skills with the ability to build trusted relationships. * Hands\-on experience with Windows environments, Active Directory, and networking fundamentals. * Knowledge of switch configuration, VLANs, IP routing, and firewall setup. * Strong documentation skills and attention to detail. * Exposure to scripting, Azure, or AWS would be beneficial. * Ability and willingness to travel internationally when required. * Experience within aviation technology is advantageous but not essential. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: IT Support Engineer (Tier 2)
description: **Overview** Viatel Technology Group is a complete Digital Services provider. We provide fully managed security, networking, cloud and communications services to servicing a wide range of clients across a variety of business sectors. Our Culture ensures a work environment where everyone can contribute equally and reach their full potential. Our System Services Team are tasked with providing first\-class support and consultation services for our tier\-1 customers helping said achieve their full ICT potential through the power of technology. We are seeking an individual to join this team, an individual who is highly motivated with a demonstrated ability to work on a variety of technologies, across a wide spectrum of customers, as well as learning new skills and procedures along the way. **Our Values** * Customers Love our Service * Excellence is our Mantra * Trust is our Force Multiplier * We Care Deeply **Key Responsibilities** * Manage tickets (incidents and service requests) assigned and ensuring high quality service with prompt response and engagement with the customer. * Resolve incidents following the incident management process within agreed SLA timeframes. * Fulfil service requests following the service request management process within the agreed SLA timeframes. * Provide technical support covering a broad range of ICT services and technologies including user endpoint device support, on premises \& Cloud infrastructure, Microsoft 365 Email, Data backup solutions, security solutions (e.g., MS Defender, Sophos) and networks. * Assist in resolving incidents with engagement with internal IT teams and 3rd party providers. * Promote, develop, and maintain a positive experience to the customers. * Ad\-hoc Remote and Internal Project work. * Additional projects as required such as preparing system and user documentation and testing. * Participate as a key technical resource in the resolution of major incidents. * Comply, maintain, and promote information security in Viatel. * Build strong and valued customer relationships with assigned customers. * Understand customer priorities and ensuring services delivered are aligned accordingly. * Ensure services deliver high levels of customer satisfaction and quality. * Identify service improvement opportunities and proactively recommend enhancements. * Develop and instil a positive, collaborative and customer\-focused culture. * Coach and support other Service Desk Engineers. * Assist in evaluating new technologies, services and processes. * Maintain and extend technical certification **Technical Requirements \& Experience** ---------------------------------------- * Minimum of 2\-3 years experience in a similar role. * Relevant experience and/or certification in: + Desktop Services - Windows OS, Microsoft 365 Desktop Services - Anti\-Virus Services (MS Defender, Sophos) + Cloud Services - Microsoft Azure - Microsoft 365 Intune, Entra ID, Defender, SharePoint, Teams + Virtualisation - Azure Virtual Desktop, VMware, vSphere + Compute, Storage \& Networking Infrastructure - HPE / Dell - Firewalls, Access Points, Switches (FortiGate, SonicWALL, Sophos) - Windows Server + Data Protection - Azure Backup/DPM, Azure Site Recovery - Commvault, Veeam * ITIL Certified or relevant experience. * Microsoft certifications in M365, Azure, Security Administration and Modern Workplace. * Knowledge of cybersecurity frameworks and compliance standards. **General Requirements:** * Ideally previous experience in a service desk in an ICT Managed Services (MSP) environment. * Focused on delivering exceptional technical support service. * Demonstrable experience in delivering technical support services meeting SLA/KPI objectives. * Ability to manage multiple customers and priorities simultaneously. * Experienced in managing and improving technical services. * Good knowledge of Microsoft Cloud, technical support services and tools, cybersecurity tools \& security best practices. * Strong communication and client\-facing skills with a customer\-first mindset. * Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills. * Highly organised with strong attention to detail. * Proactive and improvement oriented. * Professional, approachable and supportive mentor. * Strong communication, interpersonal skills and ability to lead by example. * High level of flexibility, enthusiastic, willingness to learn and develop. * Willingness to travel, if required. **What we offer:** * 23 days annual leave * Health Insurance Contribution * Pension (company matched up to 5%) * Cycle to Work * Discounted Broadband * Income Protection * Maternity/Paternity Benefit * Flexi Days during summer months

title: Institutional ETF Trading and Execution Analyst
description: **Overview** We are a leading quantitative trading firm partnering with institutional clients across global markets. We are seeking an Institutional ETF Trading and Execution Analyst to join our front\-office team. This role combines real\-time problem solving, data analysis, client interaction, and workflow optimisation in a highly quantitative, technology\-driven environment. You will work at the intersection of markets, systems, and people — ensuring trades are executed accurately and efficiently while continuously improving the tools and processes that support them. The role requires exceptional attention to detail, strong analytical capability, and the ability to communicate clearly across technical and commercial teams. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Trade Execution \& Real\-Time Decision Making * Agree and execute ETF trades with institutional clients using structured internal pricing frameworks. * Coordinate with trading teams to ensure accurate and appropriate outcomes * Manage the lifecycle of trades from agreement from pre trade to execution to post trade * Operate in a time\-sensitive environment where precision, discipline, and attention to detail are critical * Accuracy matters — small errors can have meaningful consequences in live markets * Analyse trading and operational data to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities * Develop scripts and tools to automate workflows, improve monitoring, and reduce manual processes * Contribute to building scalable processes in collaboration with Devlopment teams * Strong analytical rigour and careful handling of data are essential * Work directly with institutional clients alongside Sales coverage * Explain execution outcomes and respond to technical or operational queries with clarity and structure * Coordinate across trading, operations, sales and technology teams. You will regularly operate at the intersection of multiple teams with different priorities * The ability to communicate precisely, align stakeholders, and avoid ambiguity is fundamental to success **What We're Looking For** * Strong Quantitative problem\-solving ability * Practical programming proficiency \- beginner to intermediate in Python * Comfortable working with structured datasets * Ability to build small tools, scripts, or analytical workflows * High level of precision and attention to detail * Financial markets experience is highly beneficial * An additional language would be advantageous * Prior ETF knowledge beneficial but not required

title: Italian Billing Associate
description: **Before you apply to a job, select your language preference from the options available at the top right of this page.** Explore your next opportunity at a Fortune Global 500 organization. Envision innovative possibilities, experience our rewarding culture, and work with talented teams that help you become better every day. We know what it takes to lead UPS into tomorrow—people with a unique combination of skill \+ passion. If you have the qualities and drive to lead yourself or teams, there are roles ready to cultivate your skills and take you to the next level. **Job Description:** **FUTURE YOU supports customers and commercial integrity** Are you a natural when it comes to getting customers on your side? Do you also possess an interest in finance? If the answer to both these questions is yes, and you also have a talent for languages, you could be the candidate we’ve been searching for. We need you to secure the loyalty of our customers by showing them how swiftly we can resolve their billing issues. You’ll be making a vital commercial impact on the business, and you’ll be doing it while speaking **English and Italian language.** **What you’ll be doing** * Managing billing documents and correcting errors by issuing credit/debit notes. * Raising the bar when it comes to providing customer service on the phone and via email. * Supporting UPS with investigating our systems to prevent future billing inconsistencies. **What you’ll get** * Competitive salary * Full training to help you perform at your best * Flexible benefits – UPS Pension Scheme/ Healthcare * Life Assurance * Tuition Reimbursement * Opportunities to progress – thanks to our ‘promotion from within policy’. **What you’ll need** A university education and years of work experience are not essential here. You do need to prove you’ve spent at least one year in customer service, and experience using Microsoft Excel would be an advantage. However, we will provide you with four weeks of initial training on site, followed by a minimum of four weeks integrating you into the office team. Once you’re feeling confident enough to work on your own, you can then submit requests to work remotely until the hybrid work type model is established on the site. **How we recruit** At UPS, equal opportunities, fair treatment and an inclusive workplace environment are of utmost importance. We are committed to a policy of treating individuals fairly and recruiting, selecting, training, promoting and compensating based on merit, experience and other work–related criteria. We do not discriminate against any applicant based on age, race, religion, sex, disability, sexual orientation or gender identity. **A bit about a big business** Founded in 1907 as a messenger company in the United States, UPS has grown into a multi\-billion\-dollar corporation by clearly focusing on the goal of enabling commerce around the globe. Today, UPS is a global company with one of the most recognized and admired brands in the world. We have become the world's largest package delivery company and a leading global provider of specialized transportation and logistics services. Every day, we manage the flow of goods, funds, and information in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. Come join us! **Employee Type:** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation.

title: Junior Chef
description: Join our dynamic kitchen team and contribute to the vibrant atmosphere of our bustling cafes. As a Kitchen Team Member, you will play a crucial role in delivering exceptional service and creating memorable dining experiences. If you have a passion for food, excellent communication skills, and a strong work ethic, we want to hear from you. The ideal candidate will have the following daily duties and responsibilities: **Team Collaboration:** * Work collaboratively within the team to ensure seamless kitchen operations. * Maintain effective communication with other kitchen staff and front\-of\-house team. **Shift Flexibility:** * Flexibility to work in a seven\-day\-a\-week business, including weekends and holidays. * Availability for full\-time, permanent positions with daytime hours. **Food Preparation:** * Prepare and cook menu items in accordance with established recipes and quality standards. * Ensure food safety and hygiene practices are strictly adhered to, including compliance with HACCP standards. **Compensation and Benefits:** * Competitive rate of €15\.00 per hour. * Employee discount on food and retail products across all our cafes. * Unlimited coffee during working hours. **Qualifications:** * 1\+ years of relevant experience preferred. * Knowledge of HACCP standards. * Legal work authorisation in Ireland is required. * Driving Licence preferred. **Work Location:** In\-person at 3fe Grand Canal St, 3fe Five Points, 3fe Phibsboro, 3fe Clancy Quay and 3fe Gertrude. **Application Information:** To apply, please include your reference ID: JUNIOR in your application. **Schedule:** * 8\-hour shifts, daytime hours, with weekend availability. **Supplemental Pay:** * Tips weekly via the URocked app. **Join our Team:** Become part of a great team, enjoy the perks of working in our vibrant cafes, and contribute to an exciting and rewarding work environment. Apply today and embark on a fulfilling career in the culinary world! Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €15\.00 per hour Benefits: * Employee discount * Store discount Application question(s): * Are you currently living in Ireland? Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Junior Legal Assistant – Child Law and Social Welfare Law [Litigation]
description: **Junior Legal Assistant – Child Law and Social Welfare Law \[Litigation]** **Rogers Law Solicitors – Dublin 7** **Job Type:** Full\-Time (Monday to Thursday, 09:30 AM – 5:30 PM; Friday, 9:30 AM – 5:00 PM) **Location:** On\-site – Dublin 7 **About Us:** Rogers Law Solicitors is a well\-established law firm based in Dublin 7, specialising in various areas including but not limited to Probate, Assisted Decision Making, Personal Injury, Child Law, and Social Welfare Law. We are currently seeking a motivated **Junior Lega**l **Assistant** to join our dedicated team. **Role Overview:** The successful candidate will support our Child Law and Social Welfare Law team with both administrative and client\-facing duties. The position requires strong organisational skills, excellent written and spoken English, and a minimum of **6 months' experience working in an Ireland located Law Firm (essential)**. Please note that **this position is not recommended for FE1 candidates.** **This is a fully office\-based role**. The successful candidate must be available to work on\-site at our Dublin 7 office, Monday to Friday. **Key Responsibilities:** * Drafting letters and emails to clients and third parties * Speaking with clients over the phone in a clear, professional, and empathetic manner * Managing, organising, and updating client files and records * Preparing and lodging books in the List Room * Filing papers in the Central Office and Dublin Combined Offices * Handling daily post (receiving and sending) * Using Adobe and Microsoft Office applications, particularly Word, Outlook, and Excel * Liaising with colleagues and external parties when necessary * Attending to external tasks such as delivering and collecting documents at the Post Office, the Four Courts, the List Room, and the Law Library, as well as other ad hoc errands as required. **Requirements:** * Minimum **6 months** **of experience in an Ireland located Law Firm**(essential) * Excellent command of the **English language** (spoken and written). * Intermediate or higher proficiency in **Adobe, Microsoft Word, Outlook, and Excel.** * Strong organisational and communication skills. * Ability to multitask and work independently. * Professional and friendly manner when dealing with clients and colleagues. **Working Hours:** * Monday to Thursday: 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM * Friday: 09:30 AM – 5:00 PM * 1 hour lunch break every day. **How to Apply:** Please send your CV and a short cover letter outlining your relevant experience to **clareana@rogerslaw.ie** Pay: From €26,500\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Junior Maintenance Planner
description: **Role Summary:** * A vacancy has arisen for the role of a Junior Maintenance Planner at our client’s busy pharmaceutical site in Co. Kildare. * The Maintenance Planner is responsible for ensuring the efficient, proactive planning \& scheduling of maintenance tasks. * They act as the central link between engineering, technicians, stores \& production. **Core Duties and Responsibilities:** **Weekly Maintenance Planning** * Build and maintain plan of preventative and corrective maintenance activities. * Create and manage weekly/daily maintenance schedules * Prioritize jobs with Plant Engineer, based on safety, asset criticality, backlog, and operational needs **Work Order Management (CMMS)** * Review Approved, Confirmed and In Planning WOs daily * Ensure all work orders have clear scope, priority, duration, and parts. * Maintain data accuracy and compliance within the CMMS **Parts, Tools \& Job Kitting** * Coordinate with stores to ensure parts and consumables are available before scheduling * Pre\-kit jobs where possible * Flag long\-lead or special\-order items to engineers or procurement * Source parts, obtain quotes for ordering * Raise purchase orders when required **Documentation** * Maintain standard job plans for routine PMs. * Update CMMS when required. * Link all drawings, OEM manuals, and service reports to CMMS **Support Shutdown \& Project Planning** * Assist with planning and sequencing of shutdown jobs * Coordinate cross\-functional resource availability * Book vendors for Eng shutdown tasks while ensuring inductions, RAMS and MS are in place. **Data Integrity \& Continuous Improvement** * Analyse CMMS data: PM compliance, backlog aging, schedule adherence * Identify trends or areas for improvement on maintenance processes * Suggest changes to PM frequency, task content \& work instructions. **Cross\-Team Communication \& Escalation** * Attend and contribute to daily and weekly planning meetings * Communicate schedule changes and job priorities to technicians and managers * Escalate delays, resource conflicts, or incomplete jobs to Technician Manager **Tools \& Systems Used:** * CMMS (EAMS) * MS Excel / Planning Dashboards * Microsoft Teams / Outlook / SharePoint * Technical drawings, job plan libraries, P\&IDs **Minimum Qualifications and Experience:** * Bachelor’s degree in Science, Engineering or Business required. * Ability to create, prioritize, and manage maintenance schedules, including preventative, corrective, and shutdown maintenance. * Understands asset basics, task durations, and trades * Clear communication with technicians, engineers, stores, and ops. Effectively communicate maintenance plans, schedules, and updates to various stakeholders * Can balance reactive vs proactive needs under pressure * Comfortable in CMMS, Excel and digital scheduling tools * Works closely with engineers and peer planners to share resources

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Due to continued growth, new contracts and an extensive pipeline of work, Ireland’s largest Civil Contractor have an exciting opportunity for **Junior** **Quantity Surveyors** to join their team working on a large\-scale civil infrastructure projects in **Dublin.** Candidates will be working on residential and large civil infrastructure projects. **Role:** These include but are not limited to: * Procurement, appointment and management of subcontractors \& suppliers through to final account * Produce accurate progress accounts * Preparation and assessment of interim and final valuations for progress payments * Monitor and report on project costs * Collaborate with project teams to identify and mitigate potential risks and cost overruns * Assist the pre\-construction team in the delivery of tenders * Stay updated on industry innovations, regulations etc **Requirements:** * Bachelor’s degree in Quantity Surveying. * 1 year experience in quantity surveying within the Civil Engineering sector or college placement/ internship * Strong knowledge of construction contracts, cost control, and procurement processes. * Proven experience with NEC contracts is essential. * Proficient in the use of COINS, Primavera P6, MS Project software and MS Office suite. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong communication and negotiation abilities. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. **Salary:** * €40K – €50K neg DOE * Company Vehicle/Vehicle Allowance * Benefits Package * Pension If you are a **Junior Quantity Surveyor** considering a career move, please feel to send your CV to **N.williams@gpcireland.com** or contact **Nathan Williams** on **0861502137** for a confidential discussion. GPC will not send any applicants CVs to clients without a full discussion and prior consent. GPC is a specialist construction recruitment consultancy based in Dublin and has more than 24 years’ experience working in the recruitment of construction professionals within Ireland and Internationally.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: **Junior Quantity Surveyor – Civil Engineering Projects** A leading civil engineering contractor is seeking a Graduate Quantity Surveyor to join their growing commercial team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or an individual with up to one year's experience to develop their career within a well\-established and highly regarded contractor delivering major infrastructure projects across Dublin and surrounding areas. The successful candidate will work closely with senior commercial staff, gaining exposure to all aspects of quantity surveying on civil engineering and infrastructure projects. The role offers a combination of office and site\-based experience, supporting the delivery of projects across Dublin, Meath, and Kildare, with a flagship project based at Dublin Airport. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the preparation of project cost reports, valuations, and commercial documentation * Support the management and administration of subcontractor accounts * Assist in the preparation and assessment of subcontractor payment applications * Monitor project costs and help identify commercial risks and opportunities * Assist with the preparation and agreement of variations and final accounts * Maintain accurate commercial records and project documentation * Liaise with site management teams, subcontractors, suppliers, and clients * Support monthly cost reporting and forecasting processes * Assist with procurement activities and subcontractor packages * Ensure commercial procedures and contractual requirements are followed throughout the project lifecycle **Requirements** * Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related construction discipline * Ideally 1 year's experience in a Quantity Surveying role, although candidates with relevant work placement experience gained during college will also be considered * Previous experience within civil engineering projects is desirable * Candidates with a general construction background will also be considered * Strong numerical, analytical, and commercial awareness skills * Excellent communication and organisational abilities * Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team * Full driving licence required **What's on Offer** * Competitive salary (€40,000 – €45,000 DOE) * Company vehicle provided * Opportunity to work with a leading civil engineering contractor * Exposure to large\-scale and varied infrastructure projects, including Dublin Airport **To Apply** If you are a Graduate Quantity Surveyor or recent graduate seeking an opportunity within civil engineering and infrastructure projects, please contact Amy Maguire for a confidential discussion or send your CV. Email: a.maguire@gpcireland.com GPC will never send your CV to clients without your permission. GPC is a specialist construction and engineering recruitment consultancy based in Dublin with over 27 years’ experience placing professionals across Ireland and internationally.

title: Lawyer - Projects and Construction
description: **Title: Senior Associate** **Department: Projects and Construction** William Fry is seeking a dedicated and ambitious senior lawyer to join our dynamic Projects \& Construction team at our Dublin office. This team provides expert advice on a wide range of construction, energy, infrastructure, and procurement matters. In this role, you will engage in a variety of complex transactions across diverse sectors including commercial development, residential projects, infrastructure, transport, and energy. Additionally, you will collaborate closely with other departments, such as Real Estate, Banking, and Corporate, on construction\-related aspects of transactions, disputes, development projects, financing, refinancing, and M\&A transactions. This is an exciting opportunity to join a high\-performing, and supportive team and work with a broad client base while contributing to the firm's ongoing success. **The Role** In this role you will be responsible for * Providing advice on a variety of construction\-related legal issues, including project procurement, disputes, project management, mid\-stream issues like extensions of time (EOT) and claims, negotiation of construction contracts, and agreements related to project financing and development, as well as construction elements in M\&A transactions. * Drafting and negotiating key contracts and agreements for diverse project types. * Participating in some of Ireland's largest and most complex construction projects, engaging with large Irish and global clients, including developers, contractors, and funders. **The Person** * You have a minimum of 5 years post\-qualification experience (PQE) within a reputable law firm or in\-house legal team, with a focus on construction, energy infrastructure, and/or other major projects. * You are proficient in drafting and negotiating construction contracts (especially RIAI, NEC, and professional appointments) and related documents, with a solid understanding of market requirements. * You possess strong interpersonal skills and have the ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team. * You are client focused and have a proven track record of building and maintaining strong client relationships. * You are interested in mentoring and developing junior lawyers on the team. **About us** William Fry LLP is one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London, San Francisco, Silicon Valley and New York. We have over 310 legal and tax professionals and over 460 staff. Our client\-focused service combines technical excellence with commercial awareness and a practical, constructive approach to business issues. We advise leading domestic and international corporations, financial institutions and government organizations. William Fry is an equal opportunity Employer committed to recruiting people from diverse backgrounds and building an inclusive culture which harnesses the unique perspectives and skills of our people to deliver award winning innovative solutions to our clients. We are proud to be accredited as Investors in Diversity by the Irish Centre for Diversity. Please let us know if you require specific arrangements during the recruitment process. We'll do everything we can to support you. All information disclosed will be kept confidential.

title: MAC coordinator
description: **Job Title** MAC coordinator**Job Description Summary** **Job Description** Add Change (MAC) Coordinator at our Europe location. This role is intended to support Cushman \& Wakefield’s Project Management (PM) team, Facility Management Team, by serving as the source for MAC (Moves, Adds, \& Changes) coordination in our EUR \& EMEA region. **Role Expectations** **Vendor Coordination** * **Service Scheduling:** Plan and coordinate multiple vendors for tasks such as branding, furniture installations or removals, associate relocations, and new asset deployments. * **Onsite Escorting:** Escort vendors throughout the facility to support timely and secure service execution. * **Punch List Management:** Develop and maintain punch lists to track outstanding work and ensure task completion. **Move Strategy \& Space Management** * **Logistics Planning:** Leverage Account software to design and implement effective move strategies. Utilize headcount data, space utilization metrics, and related inputs to support planning. * **Data Analysis:** Interpret space and occupancy data to optimize workplace usage and support evolving business needs. **Documentation \& Process Improvement** * **Assessment Reporting:** Prepare detailed documentation to summarize findings and compare tactical options for space\-related decisions. * **Process Optimization:** Identify and recommend process improvements based on assessments, including cost\-benefit analysis and risk evaluations tied to facility changes or additions. **Project****/Facility****Support** * **Site Readiness:** Assist with preparation for new sites within Europe by implementing desk tags, partnering with account Tech, and AV representatives first day/week of occupancy. * **Decommission Assist**: Assist FM with gathering asset information and site due diligence. Partner with account and FM on escorting vendors **Quality Assurance** Move * **Deliverables Review:** Oversee and review MAC (Moves, Adds, Changes) project deliverables to ensure they meet Cushman \& Wakefield’s quality standards and expectations. **Experience Requirements** Candidates should have 2–5 years of relevant experience in at least two to three of the following areas: interior design, occupancy planning, small project management, facilities management, furniture specification, or logistics coordination. Knowledge in AutoCAD and/or another CAFM (Computer\-Aided Facility Management) system is required. Strong presentation development and communication skills are essential. Proficient in PowerPoint and Excel Microsoft tools. Cushman \& Wakefield is an equal opportunity / affirmative action employer. All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to ethnicity, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, marital status, or any other legally protected characteristic. Cushman \& Wakefield is committed to equity in employment, and our goal is to have a diverse, inclusive and barrier\-free workplace. If you are a person with a disability and need any other accessible accommodations during the hiring process, you are invited to bring this to the Talent Acquisition Advisor’s attention once they have made contact. INCO: “Cushman \& Wakefield”

title: Market Development Representative
description: **Company Overview** Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1\.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business\-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the \#1 company in e\-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM). What you'll do As a Market Development Representative (MDR), you will be responsible for generating qualified opportunities that progress through the sales funnel and ultimately generate new revenue to grow our presence in EMEA. This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Manager, MDR. **Responsibility** * Identify and generate new business opportunities and pipeline within a defined territory * Manage and plan your territory and outbound strategy strategically * Spend the majority of your time managing multi\-channel outbound prospecting to educate potential customers on what Docusign has to offer and gain insight into their business needs * Drive brand and product awareness in the UK/IE market through regular email and call campaigns * Schedule high\-impact appointments with those who are of good potential with Account Executives * Record prospect information in our CRM database * Present a balanced approach to cold calling, relationship development, sales process, and appointment setting Job Designation **Hybrid:** Employee divides their time between in\-office and remote work. Access to an office location is required. (Frequency: Minimum 2 days per week; may vary by team but will be weekly in\-office expectation) Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law. What you bring **Basic** * 1\+ years of related experience * Fluency in English * Experience with sales cycles and solution\-selling tactics * Experience with Salesforce CRM, Google Workspace, and other core business applications * Ability to quickly learn and adopt new technologies in a fast\-paced, AI\-first environment **Preferred** * Track record of success in outbound sales/business development role * Confident and courteous verbal and written communication skills * Excellent organizational skills * Familiarity with AI\-powered sales, research, and productivity tools, including Gong, LinkedIn, Glean, Gemini, Notebook LM * BS/BA degree or equivalent professional experience Life at Docusign **Working here** Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal. We each have a responsibility to ensure every team member has an equal opportunity to succeed, to be heard, to exchange ideas openly, to build lasting relationships, and to do the work of their life. Best of all, you will be able to feel deep pride in the work you do, because your contribution helps us make the world better than we found it. And for that, you’ll be loved by us, our customers, and the world in which we live. **Accommodation** Docusign is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need such an accommodation, or a religious accommodation, during the application process, please contact us at accommodations@docusign.com. If you experience any issues, concerns, or technical difficulties during the application process please get in touch with our Talent organization at taops@docusign.com for assistance.
